mappings.rationaleAI: Covers the full history of disease causation theories, epidemic diseases (plague, smallpox, cholera), tropical medicine, and emerging infectious diseases -- directly relevant to characteristics and causes of disease, infectious diseases, and diseases of particular bodily systems.
Immunisation covers vaccine development for polio, measles, and diphtheria, and the immune system's mechanisms -- directly relevant to biological therapy and infectious/contagious diseases.

The origins of infectious disease covers pathogen-host co-evolution, zoonotic transmission, antibiotic resistance, and emerging infections -- directly relevant to the characteristics and causes of communicable disease.

Genetic mutation covers mutations causing genetic diseases (sickle cell anemia, cystic fibrosis) and mutations' significance for understanding cancer -- directly relevant to diseases present at birth, tumours and cancers, and neurochemical disorders.

Penicillin covers the discovery and development of the first widely used antibiotic, its impact on treating bacterial infections, and antibiotic resistance -- directly relevant to chemotherapy and infectious diseases.
Louis Pasteur's germ theory, vaccines for rabies and anthrax, and establishment of microbiology are foundational to understanding disease causation, biological therapy, and infectious diseases.

Hysteria covers the medical construction of a nervous system disorder from antiquity through Charcot and Freud, conversion disorder, and the history of psychiatric classification -- relevant to disorders of the nervous system and disease detection/diagnosis.
Anaesthetics covers the history from opium through ether and chloroform to modern techniques -- directly relevant to surgical treatment and pharmacodynamic therapy.
Hormones covers insulin, thyroid hormones, and adrenal hormones and their dysfunction -- directly relevant to diseases of the endocrine system including pancreas, thyroid, and adrenals.
Genetic engineering covers gene therapy for inherited diseases and CRISPR -- directly relevant to biological therapy and diseases present at birth.
The Heart covers cardiac surgery, artificial hearts, and heart transplantation -- directly relevant to diseases of the heart and great vessels.

The four humours theory dominated disease causation thinking from Hippocrates to the 19th century, with humoral treatments including bloodletting and purging -- directly relevant to historical disease classification and diagnosis.
Bacteriophages covers phage therapy as an alternative to antibiotics for treating bacterial infections, directly relevant to biological therapy and infectious diseases.

Dorothy Hodgkin's X-ray crystallographic work on penicillin, vitamin B12, and insulin structures was foundational for pharmaceutical development -- relevant to pharmacodynamic therapy and chemotherapy.
